TextENGNew: If desperation is what it takes, so be it.
...I know nothing of navigation — no charts, no compass.
...Yet one fool charges headlong, alone, into the great ocean of the culinary age!
His name... Keiichi Maebara!!
All right!! Time to... cook!!!
My spirit is second to none!
changeReason: Old translation contains extra content not present in the Japanese source.

TextENGNew: ...And yet.
There are plenty of things in this world that determination alone cannot overcome.
...And cooking is probably the prime example.
Therefore, however superficial my preparations may be... I at least need some kind of assistance!
My mind made up, I rummaged through the kitchen cabinets.
...I was looking for a cookbook. Mom should have had a few around somewhere...
For a complete novice like me, information was the first necessity.
Just as one crossing the open seas requires a compass.
For me, a cookbook was absolutely indispensable!
...Oh, here we go.
Its dog-eared, worn-out cover inspired a strange confidence.
...All right! With this in hand...!!!
...See? Just like they say — better to leap than to deliberate! Anyone can cook like a professional chef as long as they have a cookbook~☆. God bless the cookbook~...
...With such optimistic hopes swelling in my chest,

I flipped through the pages.
...However.
...A barrage of technical jargon left the actual method completely opaque.
...Oh. You already knew this, Keiichi Maebara...
...If anyone could cook something delicious just by reading a book... there would be no such profession as a professional chef to begin with...!!
First of all, three teaspoons of the small spoon... which spoon does that even mean?!
And then there's this phrase that keeps coming up everywhere: to taste, in the right amount!!

Just how much exactly is the right amount?!
Stop using such vague abstract language!!
Drain the water... season with salt and pepper to taste...??
What the heck does "drain" mean!
changeReason: Old uses 'colander' (an object) while new uses 'drain' (an action), reflecting different interpretations.

TextENGNew:
Drain the water... like slicing through water droplets?! You can't slice liquids!!
And how much salt and pepper is supposed to go in, anyway?!
changeReason: Action differs: 'stretching' vs 'slicing' — different physical actions.

TextENGNew: Ugaaaahhhh!!
Can't you write something more beginner-friendly?!!
Yelling at a book won't help...

...Calm down, Keiichi Maebara...
Calmly, calmly...
F-For now... let's come back to the side dish later.
...The rice comes first.
Nothing can start until I've cooked the rice...
Rice, at least, I know a thing or two about.
I cooked rice over a camp stove on day trips plenty of times.

Wash the rice...
...This much water should do.
...And now... which switch is it...?
Is it this button?
Oh, the light came on!
Right, right — a fully automatic rice cooker truly is a sacred treasure...
Feeling better after getting the rice cooker started, I pulled myself together and decided to tackle the miso soup next.
On day trips, the miso soup was always the instant kind...
Well, it would probably work fine if I just dissolved the miso in boiling water.
...Boil the water... put in the miso.
...Huh?
How much should I put in?

Let me see...
...Use the appropriate amount for the number of people and to taste...?
...More vague phrasing agaaaain!!!
Fine! In that case I'll put in the 'appropriate amount'!!
I squeezed the miso bag for all I was worth and dumped about half of it into the pot!
W-Was this really going to be okay...?
Maybe this was a bit too bold...?

...Mm-hm.
They do say miso soup is best when the flavor is rich.
Come to think of it, Mom's miso soup always seems a bit thin.
Maybe this much suits me better!
I said it aloud as if to talk myself into it.
Then, convinced by my own words, I nodded to myself over and over...

All right, the rice and miso soup were (probably...) taken care of.
...Next up... the main event: the side dish!!
But... what should I make...

Something I can actually cook... that will add some variety to the meal...
...Hmm......
That's it — what about a vegetable stir-fry?!
Vegetables you can even eat raw, and surely a little heat won't kill them...
...I was too naïve to notice that I had already, quite early on, lowered my sights from "a presentable dinner" to "anything that can be consumed" — and that is entirely endearing in its own way...
There were quite a few vegetables in the refrigerator.
...Hmm, what vegetables actually go in a stir-fry...?

Bean sprouts?
Cabbage?
Chives?
Onion?
Can you put cucumber and lettuce in...?
For now, I grabbed whatever was at hand, hacked everything into rough chunks on the cutting board, and tossed it all into the wok.
This is getting quite dynamic...

The essence of men's cooking?
Hey, this is actually starting to feel fun...!
Before I knew it, the wok was heaped with vegetables.
Right, next is the cooking oil!
Glug, glug, glug went the oil...

Pouring it to the brim feels more dramatic.
...Whoops, a little spilled over...
Well, no point in fretting over minor details.
The great Keiichi Maebara cooks big!

Now let's light the burner.
......Fwoom!

Whoooaaa...
The oil brimming in the wok caught fire and the flames shot straight up to the ceiling...!!
changeReason: Location differs: 'sky' vs 'ceiling' changes the physical setting.

TextENGNew:
A-Amazing!!
I feel like some kind of Iron Chef...!!
What if I'm actually a culinary master of fire...!!!
Words are a dangerous thing...
What I was saying was completely reckless... yet speaking it aloud somehow convinced me of it...!

Gah-ha-ha-ha-ha!
Yes, I am an Iron Chef!!
...Ow, hot hot hot!!
...Right about the time the spectacular column of flame began scorching the ceiling,
I finally realized that what I had been thinking of as magnificent cooking was actually on the verge of becoming a catastrophic disaster...

Wait...
...Could it be.
...That I...
...was doing something absolutely insane...?
The fire kept growing stronger and stronger... wait, what... what's going on??

Gyaaaaahhhhh!!!
What in the world are you doing?!
Turn the gas off this instant!!

Thwack!!
Somebody slapped the back of my head with full force!
When I spun around to see what was happening, to my shock, Satoko and Rika-chan were standing there.

Eh?!
Satoko?
Rika-chan...?
I stood stunned at their unexpected appearance, and Satoko shoved me out of the way and turned off the gas.
Rika-chan soaked a cloth in water, spread it open, and draped it over the pot.
She kept soaking more cloths, layering a second, then a third...
Once enough had been piled on, the fire finally died.
...It had all happened in an instant.
Having narrowly avoided a major blaze, the three of us caught our breath, gasping for air...

...Wait, what are you two doing in my house?!
This is trespassing!!
And yet, Keiichi-san, you have been caught in the very act of arson, have you not?! This was about to become a full-scale house fire, wasn't it?!
...It would be very, very sad if Keiichi had no home to go to from today onwards.
Even the self-appointed Iron Chef... was finally beginning to grasp the situation...
...Ugh... S-So... that really was about to set the house on fire...?

Is that not obvious?!?
Just look!!
The ceiling is black with soot, is it not?!
Only a man's back is allowed to be blackened with soot!!
Satoko was genuinely furious with me.
...It was possible they had just saved my life...
When I heard what had happened...
I had been so absorbed in my infernal cooking that I hadn't noticed Satoko and Rika-chan ringing the doorbell.
But they could tell someone was home, so they came in to check on things.
...Thank goodness... they did...
...If Satoko and I hadn't come, the fire engines would be going wee-woo right about now.
Keiichi-san, do you understand now how serious this was?
Somehow, I had sat myself down in a formal kneel and was listening with appropriate gravity.

Urk...
I-I'm sorry.
...You both saved my life...

We came to see what kind of spectacular dinner you were making!
And just as we feared, it's in ruins!
At this rate, we don't even need to wait for the bento contest!

...Urk, that's mean.
...So you and Rika-chan came all the way here just to check on what I'd cook for dinner...?!
Is that not something to take to heart — that your mean, nosy visitors saved your house and your life?

...Ugh...
Ugh, yes, I am thowwily sowwy, Satoko-thsan...

After showing them such a disaster, there was no more room for bluster...
I had surrendered completely...

You tried so hard at the curry, too.
Where did that Keiichi-san go?
Really, now!
Urk... At the curry contest I had all our underclassmen helping me, and I borrowed other teams' pots outright... It's not as if I actually cooked anything...

Keiichi-san, you cheated?!?!
...Honestly, even for a club activity, using such methods is just awful!

...In my view, dumping a whole bag of salt into someone else's pot was far more awful... but at this point Satoko was holding the higher ground, and there was nothing I could say.
I dutifully hung my head.

...By the way, Keiichi-san.
...This alarming pot.
...This wasn't supposed to be miso soup, was it...?
Satoko stared at the pot packed to bursting with miso, her expression grave...
Ahhh... so I really did get the amount wrong...
Judging from Satoko's expression... what was in that pot could not, by any stretch, be called miso soup...

N-Never mind the miso soup — the r-rice should have cooked just fine!
Rice cookers these days are so convenient!
It should just about be ready...!

...Meep.
It hasn't cooked.
Look — the inside is still completely full of water.

Eh?
What?!
But I pressed the switch, and the light came on...!

...Keiichi-san.
The button you pressed...
was the timer button, you see.
It's set for breakfast, so it won't turn on until early tomorrow morning.
G-Gyaaahhhhhhh...!!

So what you're saying is?!
In the end... not only do I have no side dish, but no miso soup!
Not even rice!!
I failed to cook absolutely anything by myself?!?!

Ohhhh-ho-ho-ho-ho-ho!!
Serves you right!!
Well?
Have you finally learned your place?

Come!!
Admit it!!
Say it — I, Keiichi Maebara, am all talk and a far worse cook than Satoko Houjou-san!!

Migyaaahhhhh!!
I am a miserable wretch and a far worse cook than Satoko!!!
I humbly beg forgiveness!!!
Ugaaahhhhh!!
I writhed in agony.
changeReason: Physical action differs: 'fainted' (lost consciousness) vs 'writhed' (twisted in pain).

TextENGNew: Rika-chan patted my head with a satisfied look.
...And Satoko laughed out loud in triumph.
...Ahhh... I've been utterly defeated...
...In the end, for all my big talk, I couldn't produce a single edible dish.
At this rate, no dinner tonight...
No breakfast tomorrow morning, and no bento for lunch either.
...I didn't know how many days it would be before my parents returned... but at this rate, without any doubt...
I-I'm going to staaaarve to death...!!
And of course, no bento tomorrow either!!
And I'm the one who ends up with tomorrow's punishment game!! Gyaaaahhhhh!!!
Rika-chan wore the biggest smile imaginable and kept on and on patting my head...
Satoko, thoroughly disgusted at the pathetic sight before her, let out a long, heavy sigh.

...Grown men truly are hopeless, aren't they.
...Come on!
Would you please get out of the way!
Satoko shoved aside my sniveling self with visible displeasure, dumped the miso soup ruins into the drain rack, and began washing the pot.

D-Don't bother, Satoko...
I can at least clean up my own mess...!

I couldn't have Satoko clean up the mess I'd made.
Thinking that, I tried to stop her — and that's when Rika-chan tugged on my clothes.
...Keiichi should just watch quietly.
She said it with a smile.
Q-Quiet and watch... what do you mean, Rika-chan?!

...That is what she means.
Keiichi-san, just be quiet and leave it to me.
Satoko turned around and said it, sounding put-upon.

...As if to say that amateurs should stay out of it.
That annoyed expression was a little frightening.
Leave what to you...?
Satoko gave no answer — she spun back around and began rummaging through the pots and the refrigerator.

Hey...
Don't go digging through everything...!
If you turn the place inside out, Mom might get upset with me later...!

Do stop making such a racket!
Men are not supposed to enter the kitchen!
Rika!
...Aye aye, sir~
Satoko snapped her fingers and Rika-chan, up on her tiptoes, grabbed me by the collar and began dragging me away. Ahhh...
Leaving Satoko alone in the kitchen, Rika-chan hauled me back into the sitting room.

Hey, stop it... Rika-chan.
Let me go already...!
If we leave the kitchen to Satoko, it'll end up in ruins...!

...Today, Satoko came to show you up.
It's her revenge.

R-Revenge...?!
Come to think of it... her face did look rather grim...
Before long, sounds came from the kitchen: the squeak of a tap, the click of a gas dial.
Nostalgic sounds associated with dinnertime began to reach me — the refrigerator opening and closing, the thud of a knife on the cutting board.

So what was this?
Was Satoko... Was she cooking??

...Keiichi made so much fun of her.
Satoko wanted to show you that she can cook properly too.

``...Urk.
...Just listening to the sounds like this...
...There haven't been any crashes, pops, or explosions...''
changeReason: Sound descriptions differ: 'blood spilling' vs 'crashes/pops' — substantially different events.

TextENGNew: ...At this point, at least, she had proved herself a more capable cook than me.
The cooking contest — Satoko Houjou versus Keiichi Maebara — looked to have been settled already...
I recalled Satoko's expression from before, when she had seemed slightly displeased.

...I was being a bit too hard on Satoko today, wasn't I...?
Could she be... holding a grudge...?

I had just been in our usual back-and-forth mode, goading each other for sport.
...But I think I may have gone a bit too far...
...At the very least, I — someone who had only ever cooked for recreation — should not have been mocking Satoko, who cooked for herself every day...
...Satoko isn't really holding much of a grudge.

But she went so far out of her way to come to my house and push her way in.
...She must be angry, surely...
I quietly peeked into the kitchen.

She was nothing like her usual scatterbrained self, however you looked at it.
Her eyes were sharp and her movements crisp and efficient... it was a little unnerving.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: She must have sensed me, because she glanced back and shot me a sharp, irritated glare!
...I recoiled in fright and hastily pulled my head back.

She's mad...!
Definitely mad...!
...Ahhhh, how will she take her revenge on me...?
Aahhhhh...

...That's just Satoko being serious because she doesn't want to fail.
She isn't angry.

How can you say she isn't angry?!
I've never seen Satoko look this frightening!
Keiichi-san!! I can hear you, you know?!
S-Sorryyyy!!
I jumped at Satoko's shout and turned to face the kitchen.
...I was no different from a mischievous child called to the staff room for wrongdoing.

Where do you keep the miso?!
The refrigerator is so enormous I can't find anything in it!

S-Sorry!!
The miso is... it's in the refrigerator, middle shelf, at the back left of the lower rack...
W-What is this?

You need to close the miso package properly after you use it!
Now the inside of the refrigerator is all covered in miso, isn't it?!
Gyagya! I'm sorry!!
Before I had even found something to wipe it with, Satoko wiped it clean with a wet cloth.

Really now. Why are men always so sloppy about cleaning up after themselves?
I shudder to think what you'll be like as an adult.

Urgh.
...I couldn't argue back at Satoko today...
All I could do was lower my head meekly and apologize.
As Satoko lectured and I apologized or murmured in agreement... a pleasant aroma gradually began to fill the kitchen.

This is how you add the miso to the soup!
You scoop a little out with the ladle and dissolve it with your chopsticks!
You add it a bit at a time, tasting as you go.
Tipping the entire pack straight into the pot is absolutely out of the question!!

Urgh, I'm sorry!
I never paid any attention in home ec class...!

You're pathetic!
Really now.
Men!
...Grumble grumble.

I shrank under her disgruntled muttering.
...But watching her like this, she was actually quite a capable cook.
...It was only that she was overshadowed by Rena's and Rika-chan's exceptional skill. It was clear she had more than enough ability to pass muster.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew:
Come now, if you have time to stand there daydreaming, go and set out the bowls on the table!
Rika and I are eating too, so set three places!
Ah... On it!!
Flustered, I got the right number of dishes out of the cabinet and headed to the dining room.
When I returned, the sound of laughter from a variety program drifted from the television.
Rika-chan was sprawled on the sofa watching TV, entirely at ease.
When she noticed me arranging the dishes on the table, she came pattering over.

...Dinnertime is soon.
Dinner at Keiichi's house.
Nipah~☆.

Rika-chan seems to be in fine spirits...
Quite a contrast to Satoko's bad mood...

...Satoko is in no bad mood at all.
She's enjoying herself more than she has in a very long time.

Enjoying herself?
That, seriously?!

...She is just like she was when she and Nii-nii were together.
It really has been a very long time since I've seen Satoko like this.
...Nii-nii?

What the heck is Nii-nii?
Is it a cat's meow???

...'Nii-nii' means 'big brother.'
Satoko's older brother.
Satoko's... older brother.

...His name is Satoshi.
Haven't you heard that name before?

Satoshi.
...Satoshi Houjou?

I might have, I might not have.
...At least, he didn't go to our school.

His name is Satoshi, then.
Satoko's big brother...
...Hmm.
First I've heard of him.

She had a brother, huh.
I had no idea.
"He does," said Rika-chan, and she smiled more brightly than ever, going nipah~.

...When Satoko was with Satoshi, she always looked like she was having fun — just like this.
...It really has been a very long time since I've seen Satoko enjoy herself so much.

However I looked at Satoko, she appeared to be in a bad mood...
But her good friend Rika-chan was telling me it was just the opposite.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Is she really... enjoying herself?

...She must be remembering the time she spent with Nii-nii.
...It's nostalgic for her.

Nostalgic?
Hmm.
So he's living apart from Satoko now?
Maybe Satoko's older brother Satoshi is quite a bit older than her?
He must have long since gone out on his own and set up somewhere far away.
It sounds like Satoko and this Satoshi got along well.

...They did.
They were a very close pair of siblings.
She stretched her arms wide to show just how close.
According to Rika-chan...

...Satoko's role was to nag and support her gentle, slightly hapless older brother.
Apparently that was their relationship.

...I see.
That certainly applied to the current situation...
On one side: me, helpless and unable to cook even one dish properly. On the other: Satoko, redoing it all while scolding me.
Could it be that Satoko...
...was catching a glimpse of her brother — who now lived somewhere far away — through me?

...She would always nitpick at Satoshi just like this...
but she firmly supported him all the same, and they lived well together...
...It was Satoko who suggested it, today — coming to Keiichi's house.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew:
She said: there's no way someone as helpless as Keiichi-san could possibly manage dinner.
We have to go and make it for him.
...Then... she didn't come here for revenge, or to settle a score, or anything like that...

...She couldn't leave you to fend for yourself.
Even now, Satoko is a proper, capable little sister.
I was an only child.
I didn't know what it felt like to have siblings.
...Of course, I had never had a little sister like Satoko.

But...
For this moment at least, I felt like an older brother — obediently, helplessly being looked after by her.
No teasing. No messing around.

...I want to say something that honors all the trouble she's going to.
......That was my honest thought.

Keiichi-san!
Do you know where on earth the rice paddle is?!

Ah, sorry, sorry...!!
The paddle should be in the drawer to your right...!
I ran into the kitchen, took the paddle from the drawer, and handed it to Satoko.

Right, it's all done.
Have you put out the bowls and things?

Yeah.
Everything's ready to go.

Then let us have dinner.
Rika!
Dinner's ready!!
Rika-chan's delighted voice drifted in from the dining room.
Satoko turned off the gas burner and began carrying the pot of miso soup.

Could you bring the rice cooker, Keiichi-san?
Be careful not to flip it, all right?!
Uh, right. I'll be careful...
I can carry a rice cooker without being warned about it...
I thought about saying that, but decided against it.
No wisecracking or biting back today.
Rice billowing with steam, and miso soup.
And several side dishes.
On the table... was a thoroughly solid, homely dinner.

Nothing showy, nothing elaborate.
The daily food a housewife might make.
...Unpretentious, and brimming with the warmth of everyday life.
Let us eat.
Thank you for the meal!!
At Satoko's signal, Rika-chan and I both called out our thanks in loud voices.

...I underestimated you, Satoko.
This is a complete defeat.

Oh-ho-ho!
Have you revised your opinion of me?
I may not be as good as Rika or Rena-san, but I can cook far better than the helpless Keiichi-san!!
Yeah, yeah. I stand corrected.
The moment I honestly admitted my defeat, Satoko's expression finally softened, and she laughed in her usual loud, cheerful way.

Chomp chomp...
...Oh... The rice has turned out quite nicely.
Even with the rice cooker, the amount of water must have been different, because it tasted a little unlike Mom's usual rice.
Even so, I could sense Satoko's personality in it, and somehow it tasted better than usual.
The miso soup was on the strong side, but it was delicious all the same.

The side dishes, though not flashy, were genuinely good.
My chopsticks moved on their own.

...The side dishes are excellent too.
...Satoko, you are really not a bad cook.
I said it plainly, without the slightest trace of sarcasm.
Satoko seemed to have steeled herself for at least some sarcasm, so my frank praise instead left her taken aback.

Oh-ho-ho...!
Your praise honors me, but it's nothing so grand.

The side dishes are just things I bought ready-made and heated up.
This, and this — I simply opened the tins.
All I actually made myself was... the rice and the miso soup.
Satoko said it with faint embarrassment, a slight flush on her cheeks.
...There was none of her usual verbal barbs in those words either.
...I can't really make anything except rice and miso soup, but if you lay out some ready-made sides alongside them, it comes together into something fairly decent.

My mom uses ready-made sides and canned things too.
There's nothing to be embarrassed about.
...This is a proper dinner.

I said it sincerely.
...I've never met him, but Satoshi — Satoko's brother — would surely praise her in exactly the same way.

...Hmm.
...Well, if that's enough for you to understand that I can manage in the kitchen, then that is sufficient.

Would you like seconds?
There is still plenty of miso soup.

Huh...?
Did she just change the subject, flustered?
Realizing that my approach had been exactly right filled me with quiet happiness.
...Eating together like this feels like we're eating with Satoshi. It's nice.
Rika-chan mentioned Satoko's older brother's name again.
...Satoko's chopsticks stopped, and she looked up at the ceiling.

...How nostalgic.
I wonder where he is right now, and what he's doing.
It was a slightly faraway smile — the kind you give when missing a friend who has moved away.
...From the way it looked, I got the impression she hadn't seen Satoshi in quite some time.
...Satoko noticed I was giving her a blank look.

Ah, I do apologize.
Satoshi is my Nii-nii —

...No, I mean — !
My older brother.

Ah... right.
I see.

Nii-nii must have been an affectionate nickname only Satoko used for him.
The way she hastily corrected herself was funny.

I'm actually quite surprised someone so lacking in practical life skills had it in him to run away.
Well, I imagine he'll soon throw up his hands and come wandering back.
...So he ran away from home.
...There was another side to what Satoko had said so lightly.
...The reason for Satoshi's absence was something a little more serious.
Everyone had been speaking of him in such a cheerful tone, so I had kept the conversation going...
...But could it have been a topic that should have been left alone...?
That thought made me quickly check Satoko's expression, but... there was not a trace of the gloom I had feared.

Well, isn't dreaming of living alone just what people his age do?
Nii-nii won't be able to keep it up for long in any case.
He has no staying power.

...Satoshi may not look it, but he is a very determined person.
Far more determined than you give him credit for.

Rika, you always take Nii-nii's side straightaway.
You're a little too soft on him.

Satoko is too hard on Nii-nii.
You should be a little kinder to him when he comes back.
Satoko and Rika-chan's views on Nii-nii — that is to say, Satoshi — ran completely parallel...
But it was plain to see that both of them... were very fond of him.
And even in a parting as drastic as running away, there was such easy optimism — as if they might see him again next week.
Both Satoko and Rika-chan still cared for Satoshi... and were waiting, certain he would come back.

I had never met him.
...But I knew for certain that this person called Satoshi was a really good person.
A moment ago I had thought Satoko must be deeply hurt by her brother running away...
But she had already moved past that.
...She was still being a capable, self-sufficient little sister, as if to say: he can come back any time he's ready.
I had thought Satoko didn't think about anything and just spent her days larking about and playing pranks.
...But really she was... ............Yeah.

......?
Why are you patting my head?
I ruffled Satoko's hair roughly.

I had the wrong idea about you, Satoko.
...I didn't know you were such a capable person.

Satoko seemed to think I was mocking her and tried to bat my hand away,
...but she saw that my face held not even a trace of malice, and obediently let me keep going.

Yep.
Satoko's capable.
...She sure is.

I-I have always been capable.
I only played pranks because you always started it.
Her usual gambit, but today I wasn't taking the bait.

You're right.
You were only going along with my nonsense.
But underneath it all, you really are so capable.
...Good for you.

...I-Is that actually a compliment?
Somehow... it sort of feels like m-mockery...

I'm not mocking you at all.
I'm genuinely praising you.
...When I'm mocking you, Rika-chan is always patting your head too.

...T-That's true, isn't it!
When Rika mocks someone, she always pets their head!

...Meep☆.
The tinned sardines are delicious☆.
Rika-chan went nipaa~☆ and her smile grew brighter still.
Her attitude was so transparently obvious, yet so annoyingly charming — that both Satoko and I suddenly burst into laughter, neither one having meant to.
Laughter brightening the table.
...That the same dull, unremarkable table I ate at every day could be filled with laughter like this... it had never occurred to me.

And at that moment.
I finally realized something entirely obvious.
...A meal is better when you're laughing while you eat it, isn't it?

...Of course it is.
...Laughing makes the food taste better.

How rude!
The food tastes good because I am a good cook — obviously!
Ah-ha-ha-ha-ha-ha-ha-ha...!!
It was completely unlike time spent with family, yet somehow warm and nostalgic — a circle of togetherness.
Satoko, do you want to see Nii-nii?

I-I didn't say Nii-nii!
Please call him my brother!!
Hey, you've been saying Nii-nii this, Nii-nii that for ages.
...I smiled wryly and decided to use "brother" as she asked.
Satoko, do you want to see your brother?
Rika-chan stopped eating and looked back and forth between my face and Satoko's...
Satoko kept smiling, seeming to consider her words carefully.
...That alone conveyed everything of what Satoko felt.
In truth, she wanted him home right away.
...But she wasn't that fragile. He could do whatever he needed until he was ready.
changeReason: Character name 'Satoshi' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: In return, when he came home, she was going to give him a thorough earful.
...And then they'd have dinner together, and she'd lay out his futon.

...Well, if you ask whether I want to see him — of course I do.
It will be nearly a year soon.

...It already has been that long.
Rika-chan smiled faintly, her gaze appearing to reach somewhere far away.

Well, Nii-nii can do as he pleases.
It would certainly ease my burden if he came back a little stronger and more capable around the house.

See?
You said Nii-nii again.
...You did, indeed.

I-I did not!!
I would never call Nii-nii by his nickname!!
Things instantly snapped back to the usual lively atmosphere of lunchtime at school.
But nothing had changed about Satoko's feelings for her brother.
She was living so bravely, so solidly.

Hey, Satoshi.
...Your sister is working so hard to hold the fort while you're gone, you know.
...Do the right thing... and come home soon, all right?

...Satoko.
We should clean up soon, or we'll fall asleep.
It was while we were all watching TV together, enjoying tea after dinner.
...Rika-chan gave a big yawn and then spoke.
Satoko and I both looked at the clock and realized how late it had gotten.
No... perhaps we had both noticed already.

...We just didn't want this warm time to end.
Maybe neither of us could bring ourselves to say anything.

Oh, this will not do!
I haven't washed the dishes yet...!

Don't worry about that.
I can handle that much at least.
You made dinner — I can't ask you to do the dishes on top of that.

...Are you sure you'll be all right?
When people who aren't used to it try washing dishes, they can slip out of their soapy hands, you know...?
This girl... she's become a perfect nagging little sister without my noticing.
Endearing in one breath, flustering in the next...

Emotions all tangled up together.
I gave her a vague, wry smile.

...Keiichi can at least wash dishes.
...If you break one and hurt yourself, I'll give you pats of sympathy at school tomorrow...
...Yaaawwwwnnn...
Rika-chan yawned again, so wide you could see her back molars.

...Are you really sure it will be all right...?
Keiichi-san is surprisingly unreliable, despite appearances!

Good grief...
Looks like Keiichi stock has crashed hard in Satoko's estimation.
...Don't worry. I'll wash those dishes with no trouble.
So relax and go home.

...Actually, though — at this hour, I can't really let you two go home alone.
I'll walk you back.
Let me find my bike key...
Once you've taken us home, Keiichi-san will be the one going home by himself!

You'll be in real trouble if you get lost on the night roads and wander into the Takatsudo area, you know?
There aren't any houses or lights out there at all.
...I had been worrying about them on the night roads, but I hadn't imagined they'd be the ones worrying about me getting lost.
...Am I really that unreliable?

She's truly one to talk... but yeah.
...This feeling — it's not bad.
In the end it was decided that seeing them off was unnecessary.
I walked Satoko and Rika-chan to the gate.

Really, thanks a lot for today.
Thanks to you, I got a proper, civilized dinner.
...If Satoko hadn't come, Keiichi would be rumbling and howling right about now.

Truly!
I'm really glad we came.
They really had saved me...
I bowed to them once more with genuine gratitude.

Oh, yes.
I packed the leftover rice and side dishes into a container, so use that for your bento tomorrow.

And for breakfast tomorrow, bread and jam would be good.
Strawberry jam and peanut butter were on the middle shelf to the right of the refrigerator.
And cocoa powder was in the upper right cabinet...
I know all that, you don't need to go into such detail — I was about to say something like that, but I decided to listen humbly.
It was only then that I finally noticed that Satoko, who had appeared throughout to be in a bad mood, was in fact in a splendid one, exactly as Rika-chan had said.
...That though she nagged a great deal, she was genuinely happy.
...Then, good night.

Make sure to brush your teeth before you go to bed!
At least five minutes!

Got it, Satoko.
...I'll brush my teeth and get breakfast sorted for tomorrow.
And I won't forget the bento.

You need to wake up earlier than usual, or you won't have time to prepare breakfast and your bento.
Would you like me to call a little early in place of an alarm?

Y-You don't have to go that far.
...I'll set my alarm clock a little earlier.
...Thank you. For caring so much.

Satoko still looked as though she had more to say,
but she wound down, left me with a good night, mounted her bicycle, and vanished into the dark road.
Rika-chan went to follow, then remembered something and came back.

What's wrong, Rika-chan?
Did you forget something...?

...Keiichi gets full marks for today.
Clap clap clap.
Rika-chan suddenly complimented me.

Her small hands patting together.
...What on earth was that about?

I-I didn't do anything worth being praised for, did I...?
I nearly burned the house down, after all...

...Satoko probably felt like Nii-nii had come home.
I think she was very happy.

...Every now and then.
I would like you to keep putting up with Satoko's scolding in the future.
You're telling me to be the unreliable older brother that she can scold... in Satoshi's place?
Rika-chan said nothing — she simply smiled her nipah~☆.

...About Satoshi...
With such a wonderful little sister, what was he unhappy with?
...If I were Satoshi, I would never have run away from home.

...Satoshi had his own reasons.
...I am sure of it.
I don't know what he was unhappy about.
...But whatever his reasons, they were no excuse for leaving Satoko behind.

I hope he comes back soon.
...I probably can't keep playing the real Nii-nii's stand-in forever, you know?

...Don't worry.
You were just like Satoshi today.
Please do it again sometime.

If that would make Satoko happy.
...I'd take on that small role whenever it was needed.
...Rikaaaa~!!!
Far away, under a street lamp, Satoko had stopped her bicycle and was calling out to her friend who wasn't coming.

...Well, I'd better go.
Good night.
Yeah. Night.

...I can't take Satoshi's place, but Keiichi can.
...That is one less weight on my heart.

Leaving me with only that, Rika-chan pedaled off.
...Satoko's bicycle pulled alongside hers... and the two of them vanished in the blink of an eye.

Satoshi.
...Seriously — come home soon, all right?
...If you're much too late coming back...
I'll knock you flat.
...Until that day, let's both do our best, Satoko.

Even after the sound of their bicycles faded away.
...I stood there for a while, still seeing them off.
